title: France celebrates Bastille Day, Macron's last, with parade centering on Ukraine
subhead: The procession and airshow – this year to include troops from Kyiv allies as well as Ukrainian troops and co-pilots – comes as Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine is well into its fifth year.

ICE, the US federal immigration agency, claims another victim and sparks outrage in Maine
subhead: Joan Sebastian Guerrero, a 26-year-old Colombian national, was killed on Monday, July 13, in Biddeford. He was reportedly mistaken for another target by federal agents, similar to the case of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, who died in Houston on July 7.

title: Visualize the full extent of wildfires across Europe in recent weeks
subhead: The burned areas across the European continent have already far surpassed levels seen in recent years and are now approaching the maximum recorded in 2025.

title: Olivier Rousteing named new creative director at Paco Rabanne
subhead: 'Joining Rabanne is a tremendous honor. This is a house that has always challenged convention, transforming bold ideas into creations that have shaped fashion history,' the French designer wrote in a post on social media.

title: Ten years after the July 14 terrorist attack, Nice struggles with a painful process of remembrance
subhead: The city has found it difficult to convey the horror that left 86 people dead and more than 400 injured in 2016. Many victims feel that commemorations are 'second rate.'

title: 'We were meant to take as many jewels as we could': The confessions of two Louvre burglars
subhead: After months of silence, two men suspected of stealing the royal jewels described their involvement in detail during new interrogations in June. According to their statements, they were hired to carry out the heist for '€15,000 to €25,000.'

title: Silicon Valley's next generation feels the AI blues
subhead: In California, the rise of artificial intelligence no longer generates the unanimous enthusiasm it once did. Anxiety is mounting, especially among students and engineers, as jobs have been cut and democratic risks have grown more threatening.
